title: Add rename command
body:
Add rename command to rename a local container.

Signed-off-by: Zhou Hao <zhouhao@cn.fujitsu.com>

comments.body:
This is probably going to make things confusing, because historically we've based our copy of the container's name on the name that we initially set using the storage library, and we're probably not being careful about one or the other changing.

Any chance we could make `Rename()` a method of the builder that updates the `Container` field and also calls the builder's `store.SetNames()` method to update the names that the storage library has on file?  We could also consider resetting `Container` based on the value that the storage library has as a part of opening the builder, which would keep us from being surprised when someone else changed the storage library's set of names for the container.  What do you think?

```
➜  buildah git:(add-rename) sudo buildah containers
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
828e0b099441     *     3fd9065eaf02 docker.io/library/alpine:latest  alpine-working-container
➜  buildah git:(add-rename) sudo ./buildah rename alpine-working-container test-container
➜  buildah git:(add-rename) sudo ./buildah containers                                    
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
828e0b099441     *     3fd9065eaf02 docker.io/library/alpine:latest  alpine-working-container
➜  buildah git:(add-rename) sudo ./buildah run alpine-working-container bash
error reading build container "alpine-working-container": error reading build container: container not known
➜  buildah git:(add-rename) sudo ./buildah containers --all                 
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
828e0b099441     *     3fd9065eaf02 docker.io/library/alpine:latest  test-container
```

Updated.  After a change, Because the structure of the call is different, only the --all option displays the name of the container that has been properly modified.
You also need to update buildah.md man page.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 1215b16) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@rh-atomic-bot r+
@q384566678 I am not sure this can be done for podman.  @mheon What do you think?

:pushpin: Commit 4ce4de1 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 4ce4de109fcee95932abd69ea3aee20dd878d6f1 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
@rhatdan Not easily, Podman container configs are as immutable as I could get away with, so it would require major changes to allow name changes.
Ok I guess we could put this at the bottom of the barrell of issues.
